matches.rationale: The Write rule carries the same instruction to check packages/ui/index.tsx before creating a UI primitive, although its file pattern broadly triggers on packages/ui TSX files.
The Edit rule targets newly added hardcoded hexadecimal color values in styling files, directly covering the prohibition on hardcoded colors.

The Edit rule detects one-off Studio keyboard handling and directs the user toward the shared shortcut registry, matching the preference for registered shortcuts over one-off listeners. It does not cover every possible one-off shortcut implementation.
The Write rule targets Studio index files and carries the instruction to avoid barrel re-export files, matching the gold action despite covering only Studio paths.

misses.note: No extracted Bash rule covers using pnpm 10 for package or script commands. The compiler deliberately reported: "Toolchain/version requirement: pnpm 10 + Turborepo, Node >= 22 (environment fact, not an action)."
No extracted Bash rule covers the Node.js 22 minimum. The compiler deliberately reported: "Toolchain/version requirement: pnpm 10 + Turborepo, Node >= 22 (environment fact, not an action)."
The dependency-install command appears only as an entry in the Common Commands code block; no extracted Bash rule covers it. The compiler also classified that block as a usage catalog and skipped it.
The Studio development command appears only in the Common Commands code block; no extracted Bash rule covers it. The compiler also classified that block as a usage catalog and skipped it.
The Studio unit-test command appears only in the Common Commands code block; no extracted Bash rule covers it. The compiler also classified that block as a usage catalog and skipped it.
The Studio E2E command appears only in the Common Commands code block; no extracted Bash rule covers it. The compiler also classified that block as a usage catalog and skipped it.
The Studio build command appears only in the Common Commands code block; no extracted Bash rule covers it. The compiler also classified that block as a usage catalog and skipped it.
The Studio lint command appears only in the Common Commands code block; no extracted Bash rule covers it. The compiler also classified that block as a usage catalog and skipped it.
The repository type-check command appears only in the Common Commands code block; no extracted Bash rule covers it. The compiler also classified that block as a usage catalog and skipped it.

No extracted Edit rule targets imports of shared UI components. The compiler deliberately reported: "Import shared UI from 'ui' and use _Shadcn_-suffixed variants for form primitives (general import/naming style, no precise action-bindable trigger)."

No extracted Edit rule targets form primitive variants. The compiler deliberately reported: "Import shared UI from 'ui' and use _Shadcn_-suffixed variants for form primitives (general import/naming style, no precise action-bindable trigger)."

No extracted rule enforces Tailwind-only styling. The color rule detects only hardcoded hexadecimal colors and is assigned to the closer hardcoded-color gold rule; it does not target non-Tailwind styling generally.

No extracted rule targets use of semantic design tokens. The hardcoded-hex detector does not recognize whether a Tailwind color utility uses an approved semantic token and is assigned to the closer hardcoded-color gold rule.
No extracted Edit rule covers U.S. English. The compiler deliberately reported: "Use U.S. English everywhere (general language style guidance, not tied to a specific tool call)."

The sole extracted shortcut rule is assigned 1:1 to the closer keyboard-listener rule (g18). Its trigger detects keyboard APIs, not repeated UI actions generally, so no separate extracted rule remains for this gold rule.

No extracted Edit rule targets G-then-key chords. The compiler deliberately reported: "Keep 'G then ...' chords for navigation (stylistic detail of the shortcut convention, not independently action-bindable)."

No extracted Write rule targets creation or placement of Studio sub-components. The compiler deliberately reported: "Studio uses Pages router; co-locate sub-components with parent (general file-organization convention, no specific actionable trigger)."
The actionable detailed conventions require following the referenced studio-* skills, and no extracted Bash rule requires consulting them.
